era_context: Late Bronze Age collapse, continuing. Egypt under Ramesses III remains the dominant regional power, though facing mounting internal and external pressures. The Hittite Empire has been defunct for ~40 years. The Mycenaean world has collapsed. The Sea Peoples remain active in the eastern Mediterranean. In China, the Shang dynasty is in its final years before Zhou conquest. This is a period of significant civilizational stress and transformation across the Old World.
geographic_coverage_gaps: South Asia (limited written records for this specific year)

events.title: Ramesses III's ninth regnal year; continued administration and temple building
events.description: Ramesses III continues his reign, with ongoing temple construction and administrative activity documented in papyri and temple reliefs. This year represents the height of his power and the last major period of Egyptian monumental construction before the decline of the New Kingdom. The Medinet Habu temple complex, his mortuary temple, is substantially complete by this period and serves as a record of his reign and achievements.

sources.name: Medinet Habu temple reliefs and inscriptions

events.certainty_note: Ramesses III's regnal year 9 is well-documented in Egyptian records. Conventional chronology places this c. 1178 BCE ±2 years.

events.title: Shang dynasty final years; oracle bone divination continues
events.description: The Shang state continues its ritual and administrative functions, as evidenced by oracle bone inscriptions from Yinxu. The practice of divination using heated oracle bones remains central to Shang governance and cosmology. This year represents the continuation of Shang civilization in its final phase, before the Zhou conquest c. 1046 BCE. The oracle bones provide evidence of ongoing concerns with harvests, warfare, and ritual.
key_figures: Di Xin (last Shang king, traditional dating)
events.certainty_note: Dating is approximate; the Shang-Zhou transition is debated (±10–15 years). This year is assigned to the terminal Shang period by convention.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence for events listed, but the broader narrative of 'Bronze Age collapse' can obscure regional variation. Egypt under Ramesses III was still powerful and capable of major construction; the 'collapse' was not uniform or simultaneous across all regions. The Shang-Zhou transition date remains contested among scholars.
historiographic_note: This year is even more sparsely documented than 1139 BCE. We have no specific events recorded for 1138 BCE in any surviving source; the entries above represent the broader context and ongoing processes. Egyptian records are our primary source for this period. Chinese oracle bones provide fragmentary evidence. The absence of specific events for this year reflects the limitations of ancient record-keeping rather than historical inactivity.

graph_edges.note: Ramesses III's reign continues; this year follows the previous year's documented campaigns
